2024 QCCA 401
Cour d'appel du Québec 2024
2024-03-07
Bich J.A. Levesque J.A. Cournoyer J.A.

Introduction

1

The appellant appeals her conviction for sexual assault under s. 271 of the Criminal Code. She advances two grounds: first, that the trial judge improperly applied the framework in R. v. W. (D.); and second, that the verdict was unreasonable.

2

For the reasons that follow, we would dismiss the appeal on the first ground but allow it on the second.

The W. (D.) Framework

3

The appellant's first complaint is that the trial judge failed to expressly walk through each branch of the analysis described in R. v. W. (D.), [1991] 1 S.C.R. 742. We disagree. The framework is not a magical incantation; what matters is whether the judge applied the correct analytical principles, not whether the language of W. (D.) is recited verbatim.

The Reasonableness of the Verdict

4

The appellant's second ground requires this Court to ask whether the verdict was one that a properly instructed jury, acting judicially, could reasonably have rendered: R. v. Yebes, [1987] 2 S.C.R. 168. We hold that, on this record, the answer is no.

5

The complainant's account contains internal contradictions that were neither addressed nor reconciled by the trial judge. The corroborative evidence on which the Crown relied is, at best, neutral. In our respectful view, the conviction cannot stand on this evidentiary foundation.

Disposition

6

The appeal is allowed. The conviction is quashed. We order an acquittal.
